item.title: Why Spicy Food Feels Addictive
item.description: A look at why spicy food can feel so appealing and why some people keep coming back for more.

media:content.media:title: Why Spicy Food Feels Addictive
media:content.media:description: A look at why spicy food can feel so appealing and why some people keep coming back for more.

item.title: A reminder that feelings can be real
item.description: A short reminder to save for moments when you need reassurance that your brain isn’t making things up.

media:content.media:title: A reminder that feelings can be real
media:content.media:description: A short reminder to save for moments when you need reassurance that your brain isn’t making things up.

item.title: You Don’t Even Need to Help to Get the Reward
item.description: A brief look at how rewards can come from simple acts of kindness, even without directly helping someone.

media:content.media:title: You Don’t Even Need to Help to Get the Reward
media:content.media:description: A brief look at how rewards can come from simple acts of kindness, even without directly helping someone.

item.title: Can a Cold Shower Boost Dopamine?
item.description: This video explores the claim that a cold shower can increase dopamine by 250%.

media:content.media:title: Can a Cold Shower Boost Dopamine?
media:content.media:description: This video explores the claim that a cold shower can increase dopamine by 250%.

item.title: Future You Doesn't Get a Vote
item.description: A simple idea inspired by ancient philosophy that can help make overwhelming tasks feel more approachable, reducing procrastination without relying on motivation alone.

media:content.media:title: Future You Doesn't Get a Vote
media:content.media:description: A simple idea inspired by ancient philosophy that can help make overwhelming tasks feel more approachable, reducing procrastination without relying on motivation alone.

item.title: The non-relaxing part of meetings
item.description: If this feels familiar in a meeting, you’re not imagining it.

media:content.media:title: The non-relaxing part of meetings
media:content.media:description: If this feels familiar in a meeting, you’re not imagining it.

item.title: When your brain misses the memo
item.description: You heard the words... kind of. A relatable look at auditory processing challenges, missed details, and those moments when your brain needs subtitles for real life.

media:content.media:title: When your brain misses the memo
media:content.media:description: You heard the words... kind of. A relatable look at auditory processing challenges, missed details, and those moments when your brain needs subtitles for real life.

item.title: 5 Science-Backed Ways to Boost Dopamine
item.description: Explore five evidence-based habits that may support healthy dopamine function and help with motivation, focus, energy, and overall well-being.

media:content.media:title: 5 Science-Backed Ways to Boost Dopamine
media:content.media:description: Explore five evidence-based habits that may support healthy dopamine function and help with motivation, focus, energy, and overall well-being.

item.title: What a psychiatrist may tell you
item.description: Sometimes the most helpful advice isn't what you want to hear. A candid look at the realities of mental health, behavior change, and the small truths that can make a big difference.

media:content.media:title: What a psychiatrist may tell you
media:content.media:description: Sometimes the most helpful advice isn't what you want to hear. A candid look at the realities of mental health, behavior change, and the small truths that can make a big difference.

item.title: A familiar kind of chaos
item.description: A lighthearted look at the kind of chaos many people may recognize.

media:content.media:title: A familiar kind of chaos
media:content.media:description: A lighthearted look at the kind of chaos many people may recognize.

item.title: An Ancient Greek Trick for ADHD Procrastination
item.description: A simple ancient Greek-inspired approach that may help with ADHD procrastination, shared by Sasha Hamdani, MD.

media:content.media:title: An Ancient Greek Trick for ADHD Procrastination
media:content.media:description: A simple ancient Greek-inspired approach that may help with ADHD procrastination, shared by Sasha Hamdani, MD.

item.title: Understanding rejection sensitivity with ADHD
item.description: A brief look at how rejection sensitivity can affect people with ADHD

media:content.media:title: Understanding rejection sensitivity with ADHD
media:content.media:description: A brief look at how rejection sensitivity can affect people with ADHD

item.title: What happens to your ADHD brain when you skip meals
item.description: Skipping meals can affect focus, mood, energy, and emotional regulation. This explores why consistent nutrition can be especially important for people managing ADHD symptoms throughout the day.

media:content.media:title: What happens to your ADHD brain when you skip meals
media:content.media:description: Skipping meals can affect focus, mood, energy, and emotional regulation. This explores why consistent nutrition can be especially important for people managing ADHD symptoms throughout the day.

item.title: Why ADHD Can Lead to Kitchen Cleaning
item.description: A look at why some people with ADHD may clean the kitchen instead of starting their most important task.

media:content.media:title: Why ADHD Can Lead to Kitchen Cleaning
media:content.media:description: A look at why some people with ADHD may clean the kitchen instead of starting their most important task.

item.title: What Can Affect Kids More Than Yelling
item.description: Parenting isn't about being perfect. This explores why occasional mistakes matter less than how parents respond afterward, and why connection, repair, and communication play such an important role in a child's well-being.

media:content.media:title: What Can Affect Kids More Than Yelling
media:content.media:description: Parenting isn't about being perfect. This explores why occasional mistakes matter less than how parents respond afterward, and why connection, repair, and communication play such an important role in a child's well-being.

item.title: When the smallest thing feels overwhelming
item.description: Some days, a minor inconvenience can trigger a much bigger emotional response than expected. A look at how ADHD, hormonal changes, and emotional regulation can sometimes make everyday challenges feel overwhelming.

media:content.media:title: When the smallest thing feels overwhelming
media:content.media:description: Some days, a minor inconvenience can trigger a much bigger emotional response than expected. A look at how ADHD, hormonal changes, and emotional regulation can sometimes make everyday challenges feel overwhelming.

item.title: When Sunday starts planning for Monday
item.description: A relatable take on how some minds start preparing for Monday before Sunday is over.

media:content.media:title: When Sunday starts planning for Monday
media:content.media:description: A relatable take on how some minds start preparing for Monday before Sunday is over.

item.title: Why TV Transitions Feel Hard for ADHD Parents
item.description: A look at why turning off the TV can feel especially frustrating for ADHD parents, with a focus on everyday transitions.

media:content.media:title: Why TV Transitions Feel Hard for ADHD Parents
media:content.media:description: A look at why turning off the TV can feel especially frustrating for ADHD parents, with a focus on everyday transitions.

item.title: A 2,800-Year-Old Trick for Procrastination
item.description: A simple, time-tested approach to help reduce procrastination without relying on willpower.

media:content.media:title: A 2,800-Year-Old Trick for Procrastination
media:content.media:description: A simple, time-tested approach to help reduce procrastination without relying on willpower.

item.title: What is RSD in ADHD?
item.description: RSD, or Rejection Sensitive Dysphoria, refers to an intense emotional response to perceived rejection, criticism, or failure that many people with ADHD report experiencing. Learn why it happens and how it can affect relationships, self-esteem, and daily life.

media:content.media:title: What is RSD in ADHD?
media:content.media:description: RSD, or Rejection Sensitive Dysphoria, refers to an intense emotional response to perceived rejection, criticism, or failure that many people with ADHD report experiencing. Learn why it happens and how it can affect relationships, self-esteem, and daily life.
